high.spark: A human-directed cross-validation cycle where four AI agents review the same system and a developer manually fuses their disagreements turns out to be the same shape as your session-level flow governor — except yours removes the human from the middle of the loop.
high.source_shape: In the source methodology, four AI agents each inspect an eight-organ system independently, then a human developer reads all four outputs, identifies where the agents disagree or leave gaps, and manually decides what to investigate next. The disagreement between agents is not noise to be resolved — it is the primary signal: wherever two agents give incompatible readings, that is where the structure is genuinely underdetermined and attention is most valuable. The human's job is not to pick a winner but to treat the collision itself as a map of productive frontiers.
high.bridge: Your shared noticeboard is the analog of the developer's synthesis desk — the place where independent diagnostic outputs land and become comparable. Your dual gap-detectors are the analog of the four agents: they run independently, and the places where their readings diverge or fail to close mark the structural gaps that most need orchestration effort. The source's core insight — that disagreement between diagnostics is the productive frontier, not a problem to suppress — maps directly onto the mechanism your governor needs: rather than counting open findings, it should weight effort toward the regions where its two detectors give incommensurable readings, because that geometry is the truest signal of where the live skeleton is still incomplete. The source even validates your stopping metric: the right exit condition is marginal structural gain approaching zero, not finding-count reaching zero, which is exactly the 'earned stopping' your anchor already encodes.
high.use: Consider instrumenting your orchestration layer to explicitly track detector disagreement as a first-class signal — not just 'gap present' but 'gap contested between detectors' — and route higher re-orchestration priority to contested gaps, letting uncontested absences wait until the contested frontier closes.
high.limit: The source depends entirely on a human integrator at the fusion step: a person reads the four agent outputs, holds the incommensurabilities in mind, and decides what the next move is. That human is doing the work your live skeleton and autonomous re-orchestration layer are meant to do, which means the source has no working model for how fused disagreement becomes a self-updating structure without a mind in the middle. The 'live skeleton' — the artifact that rewrites itself as cross-connections arrive — has no analog in the source at all. The bridge holds for the diagnostic logic and the stopping criterion, but breaks precisely at the automation boundary your anchor is trying to push past.

high.source_shape: The organism repository architects AI collaboration as a body: a Brain that plans, a Spine that blocks untestable plans from advancing, Hands that execute, and a Gut that monitors. Each organ has a defined role, and work passes between them through explicit gates — the Spine, in particular, acts as a structural veto, refusing to let the system proceed when the plan lacks testable grounding. The organs run in parallel, not in sequence, so the system is always simultaneously planning, blocking, and executing.
high.bridge: Your flow governor is the Spine — it sits at the junction between divergent findings arriving on the noticeboard and the decision of what to do with them, and its job is exactly the Spine's job: block premature closure, enforce structural integrity before effort is committed. The 'Brain' role in your architecture is the allocation function that fuses incommensurable diagnostics — presence-based and absence-based signals that don't speak the same language — and decides where the next unit of effort lands. The organism's gate mechanism makes concrete what your system needs to resist: the pull toward the loudest or most recent finding rather than the finding that marks the sharpest structural gap. high.use: You might treat the Spine's gate logic as a design template for your governor's stopping and allocation rules: before any finding is acted on, require it to pass a structural provenance check — can you name the gap it fills, and is that gap still open? — so the governor is blocking on gap geometry the way the Spine blocks on testability.
high.limit: The organism's organ map is chosen once, at design time, by role assignment — Brain is always Brain, Spine is always Spine. It has no mechanism for sensing that its own structure is incomplete, no doubt meter that accumulates as diagnostics disagree, and no marginal-gain stopping rule that would let it reshape itself in response to gaps it discovers mid-run. Your flow governor is built around the same principle: the frontier isn't where findings stop arriving, it's where independent diagnostics stop agreeing.
high.source_shape: organism-core is an orchestration harness that, before acting, researches its own success criteria through six prioritized sources, then validates results against them afterward. The key mechanism is its Definition of Done engine: when those six sources conflict or go silent on a question, the system treats that inter-source disagreement as a structural signal that its knowledge is exhausted at that point — and escalates to human judgment rather than resolving the conflict by averaging or ignoring it. It also maintains a central truth store (Skelett) assembled from cross-source findings, provenance-tagged absence signals so you know why something is missing, and a lessons aggregator that promotes or demotes tools based on accumulated track record — effectively a marginal-gain sensor that watches whether the pile of evidence is still growing meaningfully.
high.bridge: Your session-level flow governor watches findings arrive on a shared noticeboard and has to decide when the live skeleton is complete enough to stop — and organism-core is a working implementation of that exact judgment loop. The Skelett maps directly to your live skeleton: both are structures assembled incrementally from cross-source findings, and both are supposed to become sensors for their own incompleteness rather than passive containers. The DoD engine's six-source disagreement signal maps to your 'disagreement between diagnostics marks the productive frontier' principle: when organism-core's sources conflict, that conflict is not a problem to resolve — it is a reading of where the structure's predictive power ends, which is precisely what your governor needs to detect in order to know where to direct agents next. The lessons aggregator's marginal-gain stopping condition — watch whether the evidence pile is still growing, not just whether it is large — maps directly to your 'absence vs exhaustion' and 'earned stopping' mechanism nodes: the system stops not when it has searched enough, but when additional search is producing diminishing structural gain.
high.use: Consider building organism-core's inter-source disagreement signal into your governor's allocation logic directly: when two independent diagnostics on the same skeleton node disagree, treat that disagreement as a gap-geometry signal that raises that node's dispatch priority — not a conflict to resolve before acting, but the clearest available pointer to where the structure is still open.
high.limit: organism-core's six sources are all goal-aware by construction — every source is queried in service of a specific Definition of Done, so every gap it can detect is a gap relative to a known task objective. Your anchor's architecture depends on two incommensurable gap-detectors: one that is goal-aware and one that is deliberately goal-blind, reading structural shape without knowing what the structure is for. organism-core has no analogue for the goal-free detector and cannot discover structural holes that fall outside the current task's success criteria — a hole that matters for reasons the DoD doesn't name is invisible to it. The 'unfillable vs under-attempted gap' distinction also has no mechanism here: organism-core demotes tools that miss, but it does not classify a gap as structurally unreachable versus merely under-resourced, which means the anchor's core allocation question — should I send more agents or accept this gap as a permanent feature of the skeleton — remains unaddressed by this source.

The separation is hard: Organism can debate, model, and stress-test indefinitely, but it cannot pull the trigger. Earned stopping, in Organism's terms, is not a judgment the reasoning layer makes — it is a condition the reasoning layer produces evidence for, and a separate layer adjudicates.

high.limit: Organism's allocation logic is a classification decision made once at intake: it selects which pre-formed team handles a problem and does not revisit that selection as the reasoning unfolds. high.spark: A Buddhist philosophy article about AI's cognitive limits describes a structure where a system must know what it does not know — and that architecture of self-declared incompleteness maps directly onto the problem of distinguishing a gap that hasn't been filled yet from one that cannot be filled at all.
high.source_shape: The article draws on Yogacara Buddhist psychology to argue that AI can function as a legitimate cognitive extension only when it is designed to recognize and announce its own epistemic ceilings — the places where it reaches the edge of what it can resolve and must stop rather than substitute. The key mechanism is what the article frames as 'critical architecture': a system that does not merely process input but actively registers where its processing hits structural limits. The warning is precise — a system that lacks this self-sensing capacity will fill gaps with its own echoes, becoming a closed loop that amplifies rather than extends thought.
high.bridge: Your flow governor faces this exact structural problem: when a finding arrives on the noticeboard, the governor must decide whether the gap it reveals is unfilled because no agent has tried yet, or unfilled because the space genuinely has no available content. The Yogacara framing maps onto this as follows — the noticeboard is the extended cognitive field, the arriving findings are sensory input into that field, and the governor is the layer that must perform the critical function the article names: distinguishing absence from exhaustion. Just as the article's 'critical architecture' requires the model to register where it hits a ceiling rather than confabulate past it, your governor needs a signal that carries provenance — not just 'nothing is here' but 'nothing is here because X was tried and returned empty' versus 'nothing is here because no one has looked.' Without that provenance, the absence signal is uninterpretable, and the governor cannot know whether to dispatch more effort or declare the gap structural. The skeleton-as-sensor framing completes the bridge: the session structure itself, as it fills in, becomes the instrument that reads its own incompleteness — the shape of what is present reveals the shape of what is missing, and the governor reads that shape rather than polling agents directly.
high.use: Consider building the absence signal as a typed record rather than a bare null — every unfilled slot carries a tag indicating whether it was never attempted, attempted and returned empty, or attempted and returned ambiguous. The governor then reads gap type, not just gap presence, and its stopping criterion becomes marginal structural gain across typed slots rather than a raw count of filled positions.
high.limit: The article's entire argument rests on a human practitioner whose judgment cannot be replaced — the AI is a scaffold around an irreplaceable teacher, and the value of admitting limits is precisely that it preserves space for that human authority. Your anchor describes a fully autonomous cycle with no practitioner in the loop, which means the anchor is structurally doing the thing the article warns against: substituting for judgment rather than extending it. There is no analog in your system for 'shinjin' — the transmission of faith and discernment that the article says must remain human — so the bridge holds for the self-sensing incompleteness mechanism but breaks completely at the question of what the system defers to when it hits a structural ceiling. In the article, the answer is the teacher; in your anchor, there is no teacher, and that gap is not a design flaw to fix but a fundamental difference in what the system is for.

high.spark: A hybrid memory system that fuses two incommensurable retrieval signals — semantic similarity and structural proximity — by explicitly acknowledging the fusion weight as a tunable prior rather than a derived truth mirrors the exact problem your flow governor faces: combining diagnostics that speak different languages into a single allocation decision.
high.source_shape: Engram indexes every stored chunk into both a vector store (Qdrant) and a knowledge graph (Neo4j) simultaneously, with each record carrying a pointer to its counterpart in the other store. When retrieving, it merges results from both with a 60/40 weighting — semantic similarity weighted slightly above structural proximity — and deduplicates by chunk ID. The weighting is not computed from first principles; it is an explicit prior that the builder acknowledges must be tuned. Crucially, the bidirectional index means an orphan state is structurally visible: a Neo4j node with no Qdrant counterpart is detectable because provenance is encoded in both directions, making absence a legible signal rather than a silent void.
high.bridge: Your flow governor must combine findings that arrive in incommensurable forms — some findings are semantically rich but structurally unanchored, others are structurally precise but semantically thin — and decide from that fusion where to direct effort next. Engram's 60/40 merger is a concrete existence proof that you can fuse two such signals without collapsing one into the other, by treating the weighting as an acknowledged prior rather than pretending the fusion is objective. The bidirectional provenance pattern maps directly onto your 'absence signal requires provenance to be interpretable' node: just as a stranded Neo4j node is detectable only because both stores encode the relationship, a gap on your noticeboard is interpretable as meaningful absence only if the system can distinguish 'no agent visited this region' from 'agents visited and found nothing.' Critically, when the literature tier and computation tier produced apparently contradictory force laws (power-law vs. exponential), the system reframed the disagreement as two projections of the same duality rather than treating it as a failure — the contradiction was the signal, not the noise.

The reframing of the power-law vs. exponential disagreement maps directly onto your node 'disagreement between diagnostics marks the productive frontier': in both systems, the place where two diagnostics produce incommensurable results is not a problem to suppress — it is a pointer at the boundary of what the current skeleton can resolve, and therefore the highest-value place to dispatch next. high.limit: quantum-worldline's three tiers are differentiated by domain (literature, computation, theory), not by epistemic stance toward the goal — every agent in the system knows the physics target, and none is deliberately prohibited from seeing it. Your flow governor holds the same shape: two diagnostics that cannot be reconciled into a single verdict, and the irreconcilability is the signal.
high.source_shape: Madhyamaka philosophy insists that no phenomenon has inherent, fixed existence — every apparent structure dissolves under analysis into dependent, empty process. Yogācāra insists the opposite move is necessary: the mind must be mapped systematically, its eight layers of consciousness catalogued, or you cannot work with it at all. Neither school defeats the other. The point is that deconstruction without construction produces paralysis, and construction without deconstruction produces false certainty — the two diagnostics are designed to disagree, and their disagreement marks exactly where the real work is.
high.bridge: Your flow governor fuses two gap detectors that operate on incommensurable logics: one goal-blind, one goal-aware. Like Madhyamaka and Yogācāra, they are not meant to converge on the same reading — a finding that looks complete to the goal-aware detector but incomplete to the goal-blind one is not a tie to be broken by majority vote. That divergence is a location: it marks the productive frontier where the session's structure is genuinely unresolved. The anchor's mechanism layer says disagreement between diagnostics is a signal, not noise — and Buddhist epistemology arrived at exactly this conclusion about its own incommensurable schools: the tension is the map. Where they agree, the territory is settled; where they disagree, that is where attention belongs.
high.use: Consider making the divergence score between your two detectors a first-class output of the governor — not a problem to suppress but a directed pointer. When goal-blind and goal-aware diagnostics split on a finding, surface that split explicitly as a frontier marker, so downstream allocation knows it is entering unresolved territory rather than a gap that simply hasn't been filled yet.
high.limit: This source is a philosophy of consciousness, not a computational architecture. It contains no noticeboard, no allocation algorithm, and no marginal-structural-gain halt condition. The Madhyamaka/Yogācāra structure maps cleanly onto the shape of your tension — incommensurable diagnostics held in productive opposition — but it offers no operational guidance on how to implement that tension as a stopping metric, how to score divergence, or how to route findings based on gap geometry. medium.spark: Gorantula's Rabbit Hole mode stops digging not when a timer runs out but when new evidence stops changing the picture — a system that measures its own saturation and uses that measurement as the exit signal.
medium.source_shape: In Rabbit Hole mode, Gorantula deploys parallel crawl agents ('Legs') that continuously surface new evidence. A Gatekeeper component watches each incoming result and asks: did this change the synthesis meaningfully? When marginal evidence gain drops below a threshold, the Gatekeeper triggers final synthesis rather than waiting for a fixed pass count or human instruction. Crucially, findings that never integrated into the main synthesis are not deleted — they are held in a Supporting Evidence buffer, visible and queryable, so the system preserves the fact that something was found but didn't fit, rather than silently discarding it.
medium.bridge: Your flow governor faces the same shape at two points. First, the halt condition: your governor needs to distinguish a gap that is genuinely exhausted from one that is merely under-attempted, and Gorantula's Gatekeeper offers a direct structural analog — measure marginal gain to the live skeleton per new finding, and treat a sustained drop in that gain as the signal that further dispatch is waste rather than progress. Second, the buffer: your governor currently has to make a binary dispatch-or-ignore decision about each incoming finding, but Gorantula's Supporting Evidence layer suggests a third path — findings that don't integrate into the skeleton on arrival are not discarded, they are parked in a persistent buffer and re-evaluated as the skeleton evolves. A gap that looked unfillable at hour one may become fillable at hour three once adjacent structure has solidified, and the buffer is what makes that re-evaluation possible without re-running the original crawl.
medium.use: Consider splitting your governor's handling into two lanes: findings above a confidence threshold integrate immediately into the skeleton, while findings below it enter a timestamped buffer that is re-evaluated on each structural update — treating the buffer not as a discard pile but as a scheduled re-attempt queue whose entries gain or lose priority as the skeleton changes around them.
medium.limit: Gorantula has no analog to your autonomous allocation brain — the component that fuses diagnostic signals to decide dynamically how many agents to dispatch per gap and when to escalate. Rabbit Hole operates on fixed pass counts or human-in-the-loop guidance; it does not model-arbitrate between competing gap hypotheses or detect frontier regions through agent disagreement. The bridge holds for the saturation-sensing halt condition and the persistent buffer pattern, but breaks entirely at the question of how to allocate dispatch resources across competing gaps in proportion to their estimated yield.

low.spark: A multi-agent system built from heterogeneous small models naturally produces divergent readings of the same situation — not because any agent was told to disagree, but because their different training origins make them see different things. Your dual gap-detectors are trying to do this deliberately; the source does it accidentally.
low.source_shape: When you assemble a multi-agent pipeline from small models with different training origins, each model carries a different implicit prior — what counts as a risk, what counts as a signal, what deserves attention. Put them on the same input and they will flag different things without any explicit instruction to diverge. The diversity is a structural byproduct of their incommensurable origins, not a designed measurement protocol. The result is a cheap form of multi-perspective coverage: you get heterogeneous readings without engineering heterogeneity directly.
low.bridge: Your dual gap-detectors — one goal-blind, one goal-aware — are trying to achieve exactly this kind of incommensurable coverage, but through deliberate architectural separation rather than emergent model diversity. The goal-blind detector corresponds to a model that has no stake in the session's objective and therefore notices structural absences the goal-aware path would overlook. The goal-aware detector corresponds to a model whose prior is shaped entirely by what the session is trying to accomplish, making it sensitive to gaps the goal-blind path would ignore. The source shows that this divergence is genuinely valuable — not just theoretically, but as an observable property of real systems — which validates the underlying premise of your dual-detector design.
low.use: Consider whether a cheap approximation of your gap-detectors could be prototyped by routing findings through two small models with meaningfully different training profiles and comparing their outputs against your shared structural skeleton — using disagreement between them as a proxy signal for where the skeleton has holes.
low.limit: The source's divergence is a simulation feature, not a self-assessment loop. None of the agents in the source are measuring the system's own structural completeness — they are playing roles in a scripted scenario, and the simulation ends on a fixed schedule, not when a gap condition is satisfied. There is no concept of a skeleton, no measurement of diminishing returns, and no allocation policy that responds to gap geometry. The bridge holds for the value of incommensurable perspectives, but breaks entirely at the question of how those perspectives get turned into a halt condition or a dynamic resource allocation decision.

Title: How to Build Multi Agent Systems with Small Models

URL Source: https://mindwiredai.com/2026/06/07/how-to-build-multi-agent-systems-with-small-models/

Published Time: 2026-06-07T08:00:00-04:00

clusters.label: Disagreement-as-signal: incommensurable diagnostics mark the productive frontier
clusters.summary: Nine cards, spanning Buddhist epistemology, multi-agent physics research, startup diagnostics, and production orchestration harnesses, converge on a single structural claim: when two independent diagnostics produce incommensurable readings of the same region, that divergence is not a tie to break — it is a coordinate. The frontier of the live skeleton is precisely where goal-blind and goal-aware detectors stop agreeing. These cards collectively suggest that the governor's allocation logic should treat inter-detector disagreement as a first-class dispatch signal, not a pre-processing problem to resolve before routing. The high-confidence cards in this cluster (P01-001, P06-001, P07-001, Sfc989c-001, S88323d-001, S2530d4-001) all arrive at this shape from independent directions, which strengthens the convergence without eliminating the open question of how disagreement is operationalized as a routing weight.

clusters.label: Provenance-tagged absence: a gap only means something when you know why it is empty
clusters.summary: These cards approach the same problem from radically different angles — a 404 error stripped of context, a cryptographic audit chain, a Buddhist 'critical architecture' that announces its own ceilings, a note-taking system that attaches metadata at write time — and all arrive at the same structural requirement: an absence signal without provenance is uninterpretable. 'Nothing is here' cannot drive allocation until it is typed as 'never attempted,' 'attempted and returned empty,' or 'attempted and returned ambiguous.' The low-confidence cards in this cluster (P04-004, P04-005, P05-002) are useful as contrast cases — they show what provenance-free absence looks like at its floor — while the high-confidence cards (Sf80a51-001, P07-002) supply concrete implementation shapes. The cluster does not resolve how provenance is attached at deposit time versus inferred retroactively.

clusters.label: Structural separation of closure-drive and openness-imperative
clusters.summary: Multiple high-confidence cards identify the same architectural danger: if the component that detects gaps is also the component that decides when the session is done, it will corrupt its own signal. ORGAN-I's layer firewall (P04-006), organism-core's temporal isolation of DoD definition from execution (S251743-001), Organism's hard separation of reasoning authority from promotion authority (S2e17fc-001), and the Buddhist upāya/realization split (P01-003) all enact the same discipline by different means: the diagnostic layer must be structurally prevented from contaminating the closure decision, or the closure drive will retroactively shape what gaps get detected. The cluster weakly converges on a design direction — split the governor into a gap-sensing surface and a structurally separate promotion gate — but the cards disagree on whether this split should be temporal (S251743-001), hierarchical (S2e17fc-001), or categorical (P04-006).

clusters.label: Earned stopping: marginal structural gain as the correct halting metric
clusters.summary: The most broadly supported cluster in the dossier. Cards from a web crawler's saturation detector (P02-001), a theater production governor (P04-001), a physics research orchestrator (Sfc989c-001), and multiple production harnesses (S88323d-001, S251743-001, S2530d4-001) all converge on the same stopping shape: the correct exit signal is marginal structural gain falling below threshold, not output volume crossing one, not a timer expiring, not agent exhaustion. The quantum-worldline card (Sfc989c-001) is the most concrete — it supplies actual percentage figures as a worked example of what 'skeleton measuring its own remaining incompleteness' looks like in a deployed system. The cluster converges strongly on the principle but only weakly on what the threshold should be or how marginal gain is computed when the skeleton is still assembling.

clusters.label: Passive record vs. active sensor: the board/detective distinction
clusters.summary: Several cards — including the detective board images, ORGAN-II's audit ring, and the visualization project — circle around the same distinction: a structure that displays incompleteness is not the same as a structure that detects it. The detective board (P01-005, P05-005, S4dd7be-001) makes gaps visible to a human eye but cannot read them itself. ORGAN-II's audit ring (P03-001) reads shape from outside but has no back-edge to redirect effort. The visualization project (S847fcf-001) recruits the viewer's perceptual apparatus but potentially suppresses the inter-detector disagreement signal the governor depends on. The cluster's use is primarily as a design test: for every governor component, ask whether it is 'board' or 'detective.' The low-confidence card P04-003 (skill library) belongs here as the most inert possible version of a passive record.

clusters.label: Noticeboard architecture: append-only ledger, dual-instrument posture, bidirectional provenance
clusters.summary: These cards address the noticeboard's internal architecture rather than the gap-detection logic that reads it. The theater system (P04-001) argues for strict append-only immutability as a prerequisite for safe concurrent dispatch. Engram (S4dd7be-002) supplies the bidirectional provenance pattern — each finding encodes both what it covers and what adjacent regions it did not reach, making orphan states structurally visible. Infoguana (Sf80a51-001) contributes write-time metadata attachment and preview-gated retrieval as a marginal-gain enforcement mechanism at the storage layer. Gorantula's buffer (P02-001) adds a third lane: findings that don't integrate immediately are parked in a timestamped re-evaluation queue rather than discarded. Together these cards sketch a noticeboard that is more than a shared surface — it is a provenance-aware, append-only, tiered-retrieval structure with explicit null-result records.

clusters.label: Low-signal cards: structural echoes without operational transfer
clusters.summary: These cards either failed to reach their source (P01-002), drew from sources with no operational transfer (P04-003, P05-003, P05-004), or supplied only a single narrow contact point before breaking (P04-002, P04-005, P05-002). Their value is primarily as contrast cases: they show what the governor's mechanisms look like when stripped of their most important features. P04-002 (heterogeneous small models producing divergent readings accidentally) is worth holding as a cheap prototyping path — routing findings through two models with different training profiles as a proxy for deliberate goal-blind/goal-aware separation. P01-004 (organism-ai's Eyes organ) and P05-004 (Gorantula's Detective Board) are leads worth re-examining in full rather than as summaries.

contradictions.description: Temporal isolation vs. concurrent fusion: S251743-001 argues the closure-drive and openness-imperative must be evaluated in separate sequential phases to prevent contamination, while P01-001, P06-001, P07-001, and S2530d4-001 argue that their real-time disagreement during concurrent operation is the primary signal the governor depends on — resolving the tension before it runs destroys the information it carries.

Holistic gestalt sensing vs. preserved inter-detector tension: S847fcf-001 proposes a 'gestalt sensor' that scores the skeleton's overall structural balance as a single value, while P01-001, P06-001, and S2530d4-001 require that inter-detector disagreement remain legible as a directed signal — a holistic score that smooths disagreement into coherence would suppress the very signal the allocation brain depends on.

Goal-blind detector as architectural requirement vs. all deployed analogs being goal-aware: P04-006, P07-002, and P01-001 treat the goal-blind diagnostic stream as load-bearing, but every high-confidence working system in the dossier (Sfc989c-001, S88323d-001, S2530d4-001, S251743-001) runs all diagnostics oriented toward a known target — none demonstrates that undirected input actually prevents self-confirmation at scale.

Structural reconfigurability after each wave (S4dd7be-001) vs. append-only immutability of the noticeboard (P04-001): rebuilding the skeleton from scratch after each wave implies that earlier entries can be reinterpreted, while strict append-only ledger semantics mean past entries never change — these two design commitments may be compatible at the skeleton layer but incompatible if the skeleton and the noticeboard are the same artifact.

opportunity_paths.description: Split the governor into a gap-sensing surface and a structurally separate promotion gate — the sensing layer accumulates disagreement signals and gap geometry, but the authority to close the session belongs to a distinct component that cannot be short-circuited by the sensing layer declaring itself satisfied.

Add a cross-session retention layer that persists resolved structural gap patterns from completed skeletons, so each new session's gap-detectors start with a cheaper prior about which regions are structurally empty by nature versus which are empty because they haven't been searched — reducing early-session allocation waste.

open_questions: How is inter-detector disagreement scored and weighted as a dispatch signal — is a single divergence on one node enough to raise priority, or does the signal require sustained disagreement across a structural region before it becomes actionable?

Can the goal-blind detector remain genuinely goal-blind once the session has accumulated enough findings to make the goal's shape inferrable from the skeleton itself — and if not, at what point does the 'goal-blind' designation become a fiction?

Is the skeleton a separate artifact from the noticeboard, or are they the same append-only log read at different levels of abstraction — and does the answer change whether structural reconfigurability after each wave is compatible with immutability?

What is the right granularity for typed null-result records — per agent, per search scope, per skeleton node — and does finer granularity produce more useful provenance or more noise for the gap-detector to filter?

If the promotion gate is structurally separate from the gap-sensing surface, what signal does the sensing surface send to the gate, and what authority does the gate have to send effort back into the sensing surface rather than simply closing or continuing?

None of the high-confidence working systems in the dossier demonstrate a goal-free diagnostic stream running alongside a goal-directed one — is the goal-blind detector a theoretically necessary architectural feature or a design assumption that could be empirically tested and possibly relaxed?

At what skeleton density does marginal structural gain become a reliable stopping metric — is the metric meaningful when the skeleton is sparse, or does it require a minimum coverage threshold before it stabilizes enough to trust?

How does the governor distinguish a productive frontier (where disagreement marks genuine underdetermination worth probing) from a permanent structural gap (where the domain simply has no available content) — and is that distinction detectable from gap geometry alone, or does it require a separate mechanism?
synthesis.recommended_next_direction: The dossier's strongest convergence sits at the intersection of two mechanisms that multiple independent high-confidence sources validate from different angles: inter-detector disagreement as a dispatch signal, and provenance-tagged absence as the interpretive layer that makes that signal actionable. A direction worth exploring is whether these two mechanisms can be designed together as a single instrumentation pass — building the typed null-result record format first (so absence has provenance from the moment it enters the noticeboard), then using that provenance to make disagreement between detectors legible as a contested-zone coordinate rather than a raw conflict. The contradiction between temporal isolation (S251743-001) and concurrent fusion (P01-001, P06-001) is the sharpest unresolved tension in the dossier, and it may be worth holding that tension explicitly rather than resolving it prematurely — the question of whether the closure-drive and openness-imperative are evaluated sequentially or concurrently is likely to determine the governor's entire internal architecture, and the cards suggest that getting this wrong in either direction has a named failure mode.
synthesis.what_would_change_the_verdict: Evidence that a deployed system with a genuinely goal-blind diagnostic stream running concurrently alongside a goal-aware one produces measurably different gap-detection outcomes than a system where all diagnostics are goal-oriented — if the goal-blind stream turns out not to surface structurally different gaps in practice, the architectural complexity of maintaining incommensurable detectors loses its primary justification.
